sections.section_title: Short title, commencement and application
sections.description: Short title of the Act as Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita 2023, commencement date and territorial application across India.

sections.section_title: Definitions
sections.description: Defines key terms used throughout the BNS including 'act', 'omission', 'injury', 'document', 'valuable security', 'will', 'animal', 'vessel', 'year', 'month', 'section', 'public', 'counterfeit' etc.

sections.section_title: General explanations
sections.description: General explanations and notes applicable throughout the Code including parts of chapters, illustrations, and provisions not limiting the generality of any provision.

sections.section_title: Punishments
sections.description: Lists the different types of punishments under BNS: death, imprisonment for life, imprisonment (rigorous or simple), forfeiture of property, and fine. Community service added as new punishment.

sections.section_title: Commutation of sentence
sections.description: Government power to commute (reduce) any sentence passed under BNS to any other punishment provided under the Code.

sections.section_title: Fraction of terms of punishment
sections.description: Where imprisonment is in terms of years or months, fractions are to be treated as whole — e.g. one-third of 7 years = 2 years 4 months.

sections.section_title: Sentence may be (in certain offences) wholly or partly rigorous or simple
sections.description: Court discretion to order sentence to be either wholly rigorous, wholly simple, or partly rigorous and partly simple imprisonment.

sections.section_title: Amount of fine
sections.description: Where no sum is expressed for fine, the amount is unlimited but shall not be excessive. Court must consider offence gravity and financial means of offender.

sections.section_title: Imprisonment for non-payment of fine
sections.description: When fine is not paid, offender may be imprisoned for a term not exceeding the maximum fixed for the offence or one-fourth of the maximum, whichever is less.

sections.section_title: Limit of imprisonment for non-payment of fine when imprisonment and fine awardable
sections.description: Where offence is punishable with both imprisonment and fine, the imprisonment for non-payment of fine cannot exceed one-quarter of the maximum imprisonment for the offence.

sections.section_title: Consecutive or concurrent sentences
sections.description: When a person is convicted at one trial of two or more offences, the court may order sentences to run concurrently or consecutively.

sections.section_title: Limit of punishment of offence made up of several offences
sections.description: When an offence is made up of parts each of which is itself an offence, the offender cannot be punished with more than for the full combined offence.

sections.section_title: Punishment of person guilty of one of several offences, the judgement stating that it is doubtful which
sections.description: Where court is certain a person is guilty of one of several offences but uncertain which, the person is liable to be punished for the lesser of those offences.

sections.section_title: Solitary confinement
sections.description: Court may order solitary confinement for any portion of imprisonment, not exceeding 3 months total. Strict limits on duration per stretch.

sections.section_title: Limit of solitary confinement
sections.description: Solitary confinement shall not exceed 14 days at a stretch, with prescribed intervals. Cannot exceed 3 months in total for any sentence.

sections.section_title: Enhanced punishment for certain offences after previous conviction
sections.description: Previous convict committing certain offences (theft, extortion, robbery etc.) may be sentenced to 10 years imprisonment and also be liable to forfeiture.

sections.section_title: Punishment of abettor
sections.description: Where no express provision is made for punishment of abetment, whoever abets an offence shall be punished with the punishment provided for that offence.

sections.section_title: Liability of the abettor when one act is abetted and a different act is done
sections.description: When an abettor abets one act and the person abetted does a different act, if the first act was likely to lead to the second, the abettor is liable for the second.

sections.section_title: Abettor when liable to cumulative punishment for act abetted and for act done
sections.description: If the act abetted and the act done are both punishable, and different, the abettor is liable to the punishment for both the abetted act and the act actually done.

sections.section_title: Liability of abettor for an effect caused by the act abetted different from that intended
sections.description: Where the act abetted causes a different effect than intended, if the abettor knew the act was likely to cause that effect, the abettor is liable.

sections.section_title: Punishment of abetment if person abetted does act with different intention or knowledge
sections.description: Abettor is liable to punishment as if the act had been done with the same intention or knowledge as the abettor had, regardless of what the principal's intention was.

sections.section_title: Punishment of abetment in cases not provided for
sections.description: Abetment of any offence for which no separate provision for abetment exists — abettor punished with imprisonment up to 1/4 of longest imprisonment prescribed for that offence.

sections.section_title: Punishment of abetment
sections.description: If abetted act is not committed, the abettor shall be punished with imprisonment up to one-fourth, or if hurt is caused as a consequence, then up to half, of the maximum.

sections.section_title: Act done by a person bound by law
sections.description: Nothing is an offence which is done by a person who is bound by law to do it, or who by reason of a mistake of fact believes themselves to be bound by law.

sections.section_title: Act of judge when acting judicially
sections.description: Nothing is an offence done by a judge acting in exercise of judicial power within the scope of authority given to him by law.

sections.section_title: Act done pursuant to the judgment or order of Court
sections.description: Nothing is an offence done by a person in pursuance of, and in conformity with, the judgment or order of a court of justice.

sections.section_title: Act done by a person justified or excused by law
sections.description: Nothing is an offence which is done by any person who is justified by law, or who by reason of a mistake of fact believes themselves to be justified by law.

sections.section_title: Accident in doing a lawful act
sections.description: Nothing is an offence which is done by accident or misfortune, without criminal intent, in the doing of a lawful act by lawful means with proper care.

sections.section_title: Act likely to cause harm but done without criminal intent to prevent other harm
sections.description: Nothing is an offence merely by reason of being done with knowledge it is likely to cause harm, if done without criminal intent and for the purpose of preventing or avoiding greater harm.

sections.section_title: Act of a child under seven years of age
sections.description: Nothing is an offence which is done by a child under seven years of age. Child below 7 is conclusively presumed to lack criminal capacity.

sections.section_title: Act of a child above seven and under twelve of immature understanding
sections.description: Nothing is an offence done by a child between 7–12 years who has not attained sufficient maturity of understanding to judge the nature and consequences of the act.

sections.section_title: Act of a person of unsound mind
sections.description: Nothing is an offence done by a person of unsound mind who, at the time of doing it, by reason of unsoundness of mind, is incapable of knowing the nature of the act or that it is wrong.

sections.section_title: Act of a person incapable of judgment by reason of intoxication caused against his will
sections.description: Nothing is an offence done by a person involuntarily intoxicated who was incapable of knowing the nature or wrongfulness of the act due to that intoxication.

sections.section_title: Offence requiring a particular intent or knowledge committed by one who is intoxicated
sections.description: When an offence has particular intent as an ingredient, the voluntary intoxication which prevented forming that intent may be considered by the court.

sections.section_title: Act not intended and not known to be likely to cause death or grievous hurt, done by consent
sections.description: If an act not intended or known to cause death/grievous hurt is done with the person's consent, or for their benefit, it is not an offence.

sections.section_title: Act done in good faith for benefit of child or insane person by guardian or caretaker
sections.description: Nothing is an offence done in good faith for the benefit of a child or insane person by or with the consent of the guardian or caretaker of such person.

sections.section_title: Consent
sections.description: Except where there is a willingness to submit to the act, consent given by a person under fear of injury, or by a person of unsound mind, or by a child, is not valid consent.

sections.section_title: Act done in good faith for benefit of a person without consent
sections.description: Nothing is an offence done in good faith for benefit of a person, even without consent, if it is impossible to obtain consent and the circumstance is so urgent that it would be unreasonable to wait.

sections.section_title: Act done in good faith for the benefit of a person by a person of more maturity
sections.description: Acts done in good faith for the benefit of a child or insane person by those with authority over them — parent, guardian, teacher — not an offence.

sections.section_title: Communication made in good faith
sections.description: Communications made in good faith to a person for their benefit, even if likely to cause harm, are not an offence — e.g., a doctor telling a patient they have a serious illness.

sections.section_title: Act causing slight harm
sections.description: Nothing is an offence if it causes, or is intended to cause, only such harm to any person as that person has consented to suffer (de minimis harm).

sections.section_title: Act done in private defence — Right of private defence of the body and of property
sections.description: Right of every person to defend their body and the body of any other person, and property of themselves or any other person, against any offence.

sections.section_title: When the right of private defence extends to causing death
sections.description: Right of private defence extends to causing death when the apprehension is of death, grievous hurt, rape, abduction, acid attack, or wrongful confinement enabling no recourse to authorities.

sections.section_title: When right of private defence of property extends to causing death
sections.description: Right of private defence of property extends to causing death when the offence is robbery, house-breaking by night, mischief by fire/explosive, or theft/mischief/trespass with apprehension of death or grievous hurt.

sections.section_title: Abetment of a thing
sections.description: A person abets the doing of a thing who: (1) instigates any person to do that thing; (2) engages in conspiracy to do it; or (3) intentionally aids by act or illegal omission.

sections.section_title: Abettor
sections.description: Definition of abettor — a person who abets an act or offence. Includes person who abets by instigation, conspiracy, or intentional aid, whether present or not at the time of the offence.

sections.section_title: Liability of abettor when different act is done
sections.description: Provisions relating to the liability of the abettor when a different act from what was abetted is done, or when the abetted act has a different effect.

sections.section_title: Abettor present when offence committed
sections.description: Where abettor is present at the time and place of offence, the abettor is held to be guilty of that offence to the same extent as if they had committed it themselves.

sections.section_title: Abetment in India of offences outside India
sections.description: A person in India who abets the commission of an offence outside India — if the act would be an offence if committed in India — is guilty of abetment.

sections.section_title: Abetment outside India for offence in India
sections.description: Any person outside India who abets an offence committed in India shall be dealt with as if the abetment had been committed within India.

sections.section_title: Punishment of abetment if the act abetted is committed in consequence, and where no express provision is made
sections.description: If act abetted is committed in consequence of the abetment, and no express provision exists, the abettor is punished with the same punishment as for the offence.

sections.section_title: Punishment of abetment if person abetted does act with different intention
sections.description: The abettor is punished as if the act had been done with the same intention as the abettor, even if the person who did it had different intentions.

sections.section_title: Punishment of abetment if act causing greater harm done because of ignorance
sections.description: Where an abetted act causes greater harm due to ignorance of the person abetted, the abettor is not liable for the greater harm unless they had knowledge.

sections.section_title: Abettor liable for cumulative punishment
sections.description: Abettor is liable to punishment for the act abetted as well as for any additional act done as a probable consequence, resulting in cumulative punishment.

sections.section_title: Liability of abettor for effect caused which was intended to be prevented
sections.description: When the abettor instigates another to do an act, and the person abetted does something to prevent the effect, but the effect still results — the abettor is still liable.

sections.section_title: Abetment of abetment
sections.description: Whoever abets an abetment is guilty of the original offence as if they had directly abetted the main act.

sections.section_title: Abetment of offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life
sections.description: Whoever abets an offence punishable with death or life imprisonment — if no express provision — shall be punished with up to 7 years imprisonment and fine, or if hurt caused, up to 14 years.

sections.section_title: Abetment of offence punishable with imprisonment
sections.description: Abetment of any offence punishable with imprisonment — abettor is punished with up to one-fourth of the maximum imprisonment if the act is not committed.

sections.section_title: Abetting commission of offence by the public generally or by a number exceeding ten
sections.description: Whoever abets the commission of an offence by the public generally, or by any number exceeding ten persons, shall be punished with imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Concealing design to commit offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life
sections.description: Whoever knowing of the design to commit an offence punishable with death or life imprisonment fails to give timely notice to authorities shall be punished with up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Criminal conspiracy
sections.description: When two or more persons agree to do an illegal act or to do a legal act by illegal means — each of them is a party to criminal conspiracy. Agreement itself is the offence.

sections.section_title: Punishment of criminal conspiracy
sections.description: Punishment for criminal conspiracy: if to commit offence punishable with death/life/rigorous imprisonment ≥2 years, same as abetment. For other conspiracies, up to 6 months or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Waging war against the Government of India
sections.description: Whoever wages war, or attempts to wage war, or abets waging of war against the Government of India shall be punished with death or imprisonment for life and fine.

sections.section_title: Conspiracy to commit offences punishable by section 147
sections.description: Whoever conspires to commit offences under Section 147 (waging war against India) shall be punished with imprisonment for life or 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Collecting arms, etc., with intention of waging war against the Government of India
sections.description: Whoever collects men, arms or ammunition, or prepares to wage war against the Government of India — imprisonment for life or up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Concealing with intent to facilitate design to wage war
sections.description: Whoever by concealment facilitates the design of any person to wage war against India shall be punished with imprisonment up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Assaulting President, Governor, etc., with intent to compel or restrain the exercise of any lawful power
sections.description: Assaulting or wrongfully restraining the President, Vice-President, Governor or Lt. Governor to compel or restrain exercise of lawful functions — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Act endangering sovereignty, unity and integrity of India
sections.description: Whoever, purposely or knowingly, by words, signs, or visible representation, excites or attempts to excite, secession, armed rebellion, or subversive activities endangering sovereignty or unity of India — imprisonment for life or 7 years.

sections.section_title: Waging war against authority of any Magistrate
sections.description: Whoever wages war against the authority of any magistrate shall be punished with imprisonment for life or up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Building, etc., used for human habitation in which worship is performed
sections.description: Depredation (plunder) on territories in alliance or at peace with India — imprisonment up to 7 years and fine and restitution.

sections.section_title: Receiving property taken by war or depredation
sections.description: Receiving property plundered from territories of allied powers — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Public servant voluntarily allowing prisoner of state to escape
sections.description: Public servant allowing state prisoner or prisoner of war to escape — imprisonment for life or up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Public servant negligently suffering such prisoner to escape
sections.description: Public servant negligently allowing state prisoner to escape — imprisonment up to 3 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Aiding escape of, rescuing or harbouring such prisoner
sections.description: Aiding escape, rescuing, or harbouring a state prisoner or prisoner of war — up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Wearing garb or carrying token used by soldiers, police, etc.
sections.description: Wearing garb of soldier/police/public servant or carrying token used by them with fraudulent intent — up to 3 months or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Personating a public servant
sections.description: Pretending to be a public servant and exercising or purporting to exercise functions of a public servant — up to 2 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Abetting mutiny, or attempting to seduce a soldier, sailor or airman from his duty
sections.description: Abetting mutiny or seducing a soldier/sailor/airman from duty or allegiance — punishable with death or imprisonment for life or up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Abetment of mutiny, if mutiny is committed in consequence
sections.description: If mutiny is committed in consequence of abetment — the abettor shall be punished with death or imprisonment for life.

sections.section_title: Abetment of assault by soldier, sailor or airman on his superior officer
sections.description: Abetting assault by military/naval/air force person on superior officer — up to 3 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Abetment of such assault, if assault committed
sections.description: If the assault is actually committed in consequence of the abetment — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Abetment of desertion of soldier, sailor or airman
sections.description: Whoever abets the desertion of any soldier, sailor, or airman from service — up to 2 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Harbouring deserter
sections.description: Whoever harbours any person knowing that person to be a deserter from the armed forces — up to 2 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Deserter concealed on board merchant vessel through negligence of master
sections.description: Master of a merchant vessel found to have concealed a deserter — fine up to five hundred rupees.

sections.section_title: Abetment of act of insubordination by soldier, sailor or airman
sections.description: Abetting insubordination by military personnel — up to 6 months or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Unlawful assembly
sections.description: Assembly of 5 or more persons whose common object is to use criminal force or show of force to central/state government, or to resist execution of law, or commit any mischief/criminal trespass/other offence.

sections.section_title: Being a member of unlawful assembly
sections.description: Whoever joins or continues to be a member of an unlawful assembly knowing it is unlawful — up to 6 months or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Rioting
sections.description: Whenever force or violence is used by an unlawful assembly in prosecution of the common object of such assembly, every member of that assembly is guilty of rioting.

sections.section_title: Rioting armed with deadly weapon
sections.description: Rioting while armed with a deadly weapon or anything which used as a weapon of offence is likely to cause death — up to 3 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Punishment of rioting
sections.description: Punishment for rioting: up to 2 years and fine. Enhanced provisions for rioting with deadly weapon.

sections.section_title: Wantonly giving provocation with intent to cause riot
sections.description: Whoever maliciously and wantonly gives provocation to any person, intending or knowing it to be likely to cause a riot — up to 1 year or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Promoting enmity between groups on grounds of religion, race, place of birth, residence, language etc.
sections.description: Promoting enmity/hatred/ill will between different groups on grounds of religion, race, place of birth, residence, language or caste by words/signs/visible representation — up to 3 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Imputations, assertions prejudicial to national integration
sections.description: Making assertions prejudicial to maintenance of harmony or likely to disturb public tranquility between different religious/racial/caste groups — up to 3 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Statement creating or promoting enmity, hatred or ill-will between classes
sections.description: Publishing or circulating any statement promoting enmity between different classes of citizens — up to 3 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Assertions prejudicial to national integration by a person in charge of a place of worship
sections.description: Person in charge of a place of worship making assertions prejudicial to national integration — enhanced punishment up to 5 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Deliberate and malicious acts, intended to outrage religious feelings
sections.description: Deliberately and maliciously outraging the religious feelings of any class by insulting its religion or religious beliefs — up to 3 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Disturbing religious assembly
sections.description: Voluntarily causing disturbance to any assembly lawfully engaged in the performance of religious worship or religious ceremonies — up to 1 year or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Trespassing on burial places, etc.
sections.description: Committing trespass in any place of worship or burial or funeral rites place with intention of wounding feelings or insulting religion — up to 1 year or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Uttering words, etc., with deliberate intent to wound religious feelings
sections.description: Uttering any word, making any sound in hearing of any person, or making any gesture in sight of any person with deliberate intent to wound religious feelings — up to 1 year or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Misconduct in assemblies and processions and disturbing meetings
sections.description: Causing obstruction or annoyance to persons assembled for lawful worship or any lawful purpose by indecent behaviour — up to 1 year or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Public servant disobeying law with intent to cause injury to any person
sections.description: Public servant knowingly disobeying any direction of the law — intending or knowing it is likely to cause injury — up to 2 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Public servant framing an incorrect document with intent to cause injury
sections.description: Public servant framing an incorrect record or writing knowing it to be incorrect, with intent to cause injury — up to 3 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Public servant in judicial proceeding corruptly making report, etc.
sections.description: Public servant corruptly making or pronouncing a report, order, verdict or decision in judicial or quasi-judicial proceedings — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Commitment for trial or confinement by person having authority who knows that he is acting contrary to law
sections.description: Committing a person for trial or confining a person knowing such commitment/confinement is contrary to law — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Intentional omission to apprehend, on part of public servant bound to apprehend
sections.description: Public servant legally bound to apprehend intentionally omitting to do so — up to 3 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Escape from confinement or custody negligently suffered by public servant
sections.description: Public servant negligently permitting prisoner in lawful custody to escape — up to 2 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Intentional omission to apprehend on the part of public servant in whose custody accused is
sections.description: Public servant intentionally allowing prisoner in their custody to escape — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: False charge of offence made with intent to injure
sections.description: Instituting criminal proceedings against any person knowing that there is no just or lawful ground for such proceedings — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Harbouring offender
sections.description: Harboring or sheltering any person known to be an offender — with intent to screen them from punishment or legal process — punishable based on offence harboured.

sections.section_title: Taking gift, etc., to screen an offender from punishment
sections.description: Accepting any gratification to screen any person from legal punishment or refrain from prosecuting — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Offering gift or restoration of property in consideration of screening offender
sections.description: Offering any gratification to screen any person from legal punishment — up to 7 years and fine. Both giving and receiving corrupt.

sections.section_title: Taking gift to help recover stolen property etc.
sections.description: Taking any gratification under pretence of helping recover stolen property or restoring the object of any other offence — up to 2 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Harbouring offender who has escaped from custody, or whose apprehension has been ordered
sections.description: Harbouring any person who has escaped from custody or ordered to be apprehended — punishment based on offence of escaped person.

sections.section_title: Public servant disobeying direction of law with intent to save person from punishment or property from forfeiture
sections.description: Public servant disobeying law intending to save any person from legal punishment or any property from legal forfeiture — up to 2 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Public servant framing incorrect record or writing to save person from punishment
sections.description: Public servant framing incorrect record with intent to save person from punishment or property from forfeiture — up to 3 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Public servant in judicial proceeding corruptly making report contrary to law
sections.description: Public servant corruptly making contrary to law any report, order, verdict or decision in judicial proceeding — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Commitment for trial or confinement by person not having authority
sections.description: Committing a person for trial or confining them without authority knowing such commitment is illegal — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Intentional omission to apprehend on part of public servant bound to apprehend person charged with offence
sections.description: Public servant legally bound to apprehend a person charged with an offence punishable with death or life imprisonment, intentionally omitting to do so — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Intentional omission to apprehend on part of public servant
sections.description: Public servant intentionally omitting to apprehend a person — general provision for lesser offences — up to 3 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Escape from confinement negligently suffered by public servant
sections.description: Public servant negligently suffering any person in their custody to escape — up to 2 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Resistance or obstruction by a person to his lawful apprehension
sections.description: Resisting or obstructing lawful apprehension of oneself — up to 2 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Resistance or obstruction to lawful apprehension of another person
sections.description: Resisting or obstructing lawful apprehension of any other person — up to 2 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Unlawful return from transportation
sections.description: Person sentenced to transportation returning to India before completion of lawful sentence without government order — imprisonment for life.

sections.section_title: Violation of condition of remission of punishment
sections.description: Violating any condition on which a sentence was remitted — imprisonment for remaining term.

sections.section_title: Intentional insult or interruption to public servant sitting in judicial proceeding
sections.description: Intentionally insulting or interrupting a public servant sitting in any stage of judicial proceeding — up to 1 year or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Personation of assessor
sections.description: Personating a juror or assessor, or voluntarily serving as such knowing one is not entitled to do so — up to 2 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Bribery
sections.description: Giving, offering or accepting gratification to induce or reward any person for exercising their electoral right — up to 1 year or fine or both. Election bribery.

sections.section_title: Undue influence at elections
sections.description: Using or threatening to use criminal force to impede or compel the free exercise of electoral rights — up to 1 year or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Personation at elections
sections.description: Voting or attempting to vote as some other person, whether living or dead, or claiming to be a person whose name appears on electoral roll — up to 1 year or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Failure to keep election accounts
sections.description: Failure to keep account of election expenses as required by law — fine up to five hundred rupees.

sections.section_title: Bribery offences in connection with elections — Explanations
sections.description: Explanations relating to bribery and undue influence in elections — defining candidate, electoral right and related terms.

sections.section_title: Giving false evidence
sections.description: Giving false evidence at any stage of judicial proceeding or fabricating false evidence — up to 7 years and fine; if capital offence, up to 10 years.

sections.section_title: Fabricating false evidence
sections.description: Making any false entry in a book, forging a document, making false statement/sign/seal/mark with intent to make false evidence appear as genuine — same punishment as giving false evidence.

sections.section_title: Punishment for false evidence
sections.description: Express punishment section for giving or fabricating false evidence: imprisonment up to 7 years and fine; if given in capital proceedings, up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Giving or fabricating false evidence with intent to procure conviction
sections.description: Giving or fabricating false evidence intending to procure the conviction of any person of a capital offence — if innocent person convicted and executed as a result, death sentence or life imprisonment.

sections.section_title: Threat of injury to person summoned to give evidence
sections.description: Threatening a person summoned as a witness to give false evidence or prevent giving evidence — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Causing disappearance of evidence of offence committed
sections.description: Causing disappearance of evidence of offence or screening offender from legal punishment — punishable to the same degree as the offence in question.

sections.section_title: Intentional omission to give information of offence by person bound to inform
sections.description: Persons legally bound to report an offence intentionally omitting to report it — up to 6 months or fine or both.

sections.section_title: False information with intent to cause public servant to use his lawful power to the injury of another person
sections.description: Giving false information to a public servant, knowing it is false, intending or knowing it is likely to cause public servant to use lawful power to injure another — up to 1 year or fine or both.

sections.section_title: False statement on oath or affirmation to public servant or person authorised to administer oath
sections.description: Making a false statement on oath or solemn affirmation to a public servant or authorized person — up to 3 years and fine.

sections.section_title: False information, with intent to cause public servant to use his lawful power to the injury of another person
sections.description: Comprehensive provision on giving false information to public servant to misuse official power and injure another — up to 1 year or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Refusing oath or affirmation when duly required to take oath by public servant
sections.description: Refusing to bind oneself by oath or affirmation when lawfully required by public servant to do so — up to 6 months or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Refusing to answer question asked by public servant authorised to question
sections.description: Refusing to answer any question demanded by lawful authority in accordance with law — up to 6 months or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Refusing to sign statement
sections.description: Refusing to sign a statement made by oneself when required by law to do so by a public servant — up to 3 months or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Preventing service of summons or other proceeding, or preventing publication thereof
sections.description: Preventing service of any summons, notice, order or proclamation — or preventing publication of court order — up to 1 month or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Non-attendance in obedience to an order from public servant
sections.description: Failing to attend a place or remain in attendance in response to an official order — up to 1 month or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Omission to produce document to public servant by person legally bound to produce it
sections.description: Omitting to produce a document which a person is legally bound to produce to a public servant — up to 1 month or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Omission to give notice or information to public servant by person legally bound to give it
sections.description: Omitting to give notice or information which a person is legally bound to give — up to 1 month or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Furnishing false information
sections.description: Knowingly furnishing false information to any public servant — up to 6 months or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Refusing to answer public servant authorised to question
sections.description: Failure to answer when questioned by lawfully authorised public servant on matters within their official competence.

sections.section_title: Obstructing sale of property offered for sale by authority of public servant
sections.description: Obstructing any sale of property lawfully exposed to sale by a public servant — up to 1 month or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Illegal purchase or bid for property offered for sale by authority of public servant
sections.description: Purchasing or bidding for property sold at official auction knowing that one is prohibited from doing so — up to 1 month or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Obstructing public servant in discharge of public functions
sections.description: Voluntarily obstructing a public servant in the discharge of public functions — up to 3 months or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Omission to assist public servant when bound by law to give assistance
sections.description: Omitting to assist a public servant when legally bound to do so — up to 1 month or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Disobedience to order duly promulgated by public servant
sections.description: Disobeying a legal order promulgated by a public servant knowing it was issued to prevent obstruction of public ways, injury or danger — up to 6 months or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Threat of injury to public servant
sections.description: Threatening a public servant with injury to themselves or their relations, to induce such servant to do or omit any official act — up to 2 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Threat of injury to induce person to refrain from applying for protection to public servant
sections.description: Threatening any person to induce them to refrain from making any complaint or legal proceeding, or from giving evidence — up to 1 year or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Counterfeiting coin
sections.description: Counterfeiting any coin which is current in India or any country — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Counterfeiting Indian coin
sections.description: Counterfeiting any Indian coin — imprisonment for life or up to 10 years and fine. Enhanced for Indian currency.

sections.section_title: Making or selling instrument for counterfeiting coin
sections.description: Making or selling any instrument or material for counterfeiting coin — up to 3 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Making or selling instrument for counterfeiting Indian coin
sections.description: Making or selling instrument for counterfeiting Indian coin — up to 7 years and fine. Enhanced for Indian coins.

sections.section_title: Possession of instrument or material for counterfeiting coin
sections.description: Having in possession any instrument or material knowing it is intended to be used for counterfeiting coin — up to 3 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Abetting in India the counterfeiting out of India of coin
sections.description: Abetting, in India, the counterfeiting of coins outside India — same punishment as for counterfeiting.

sections.section_title: Import or export of counterfeit coin
sections.description: Importing or exporting counterfeit coin knowing it to be counterfeit — up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Delivery of coin possessed with knowledge that it is counterfeit
sections.description: Delivering any coin as genuine knowing it to be counterfeit — up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Delivery of Indian coin possessed with knowledge that it is counterfeit
sections.description: Delivering Indian coin as genuine knowing it to be counterfeit — imprisonment for life or up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Possession of counterfeit coin by person who knew it to be counterfeit when he became possessed thereof
sections.description: Knowingly possessing counterfeit coin intending to use or deliver it as genuine — up to 5 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Person employed in mint causing coin to be of different weight or composition from that fixed by law
sections.description: Mint employee fraudulently making coin of different weight or composition than legally fixed — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Fraudulently or dishonestly diminishing weight or altering composition of coin
sections.description: Fraudulently diminishing the weight or altering the composition of any coin — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Altering appearance of coin with intent that it shall pass as a coin of a different description
sections.description: Altering the appearance of a coin to make it pass as a coin of a different value — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Delivery of coin as a different description of coin
sections.description: Knowingly delivering a coin as a coin of a different denomination — up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Possession of coin by person who knew it to be altered
sections.description: Knowingly having altered coin in possession with intent to use or deliver it as of a different denomination — up to 5 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Delivery of coin as genuine which is known to be altered
sections.description: Delivering an altered coin as genuine not knowing when first received that it was counterfeit — up to 2 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Counterfeiting Government stamp
sections.description: Counterfeiting any stamp issued by government for the purpose of revenue — up to life imprisonment or fine.

sections.section_title: Having possession of instrument or material for counterfeiting Government stamp
sections.description: Having in possession instruments or materials for counterfeiting government stamp — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Making or selling instrument for counterfeiting Government stamp
sections.description: Making, selling, or disposing of any instrument for counterfeiting a government stamp — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Using false weight or measure
sections.description: Using any false weight or false measure of length or capacity, or fraudulently using any genuine weight or measure — up to 1 year or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Fraudulent use of false instrument for weighing
sections.description: Fraudulently using any instrument of weighing which is false — up to 1 year or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Being in possession of false weight or measure
sections.description: Having in possession any instrument of weighing or measure of length or capacity which is false — up to 1 year or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Making or selling false weight or measure
sections.description: Making, selling, or disposing of any instrument of weighing or any weight or measure which is false — up to 1 year or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Adulteration of food or drink
sections.description: Making any article of food or drink unfit for consumption by adulterating it — up to 6 months or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Sale of noxious food or drink
sections.description: Selling any article of food or drink as wholesome which the seller knows to be harmful to health — up to 6 months or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Adulteration of drugs
sections.description: Adulterating any drug or medical preparation so as to lessen its efficacy or change its operation or make it noxious — up to 6 months or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Sale of adulterated drugs
sections.description: Selling any drug or medical preparation knowing it to be adulterated — up to 6 months or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Fouling water of public spring or reservoir
sections.description: Voluntarily corrupting or fouling the water of any public spring or reservoir so as to make it less fit for the purpose for which it is ordinarily used — up to 3 months or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Making atmosphere noxious to health
sections.description: Voluntarily vitiating the atmosphere in any place so as to make it noxious to the health of persons in general — up to 6 months or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Rash driving or riding on a public way
sections.description: Whoever drives any vehicle, or rides, on any public way in a rash or negligent manner — up to 1 month or fine.

sections.section_title: Rash navigation of vessel
sections.description: Whoever navigates any vessel in a rash or negligent manner — up to 6 months or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Exhibition of false light, mark or buoy
sections.description: Exhibiting any false light, mark or buoy, intending to mislead any navigator — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Conveying person by water for hire in unsafe or overloaded vessel
sections.description: Conveying persons by water for hire in a vessel which is unsafe or overloaded — up to 6 months or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Danger or obstruction in public way or line of navigation
sections.description: Doing any act or omitting to take order with any property in possession so as to cause danger, obstruction or injury to public or to navigational way — up to 3 months or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Negligent conduct with respect to poisonous substance
sections.description: Whoever does any act with any poisonous substance so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life or likely to cause hurt or injury — up to 6 months or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Negligent conduct with respect to fire or combustible matter
sections.description: Rashly or negligently handling fire or combustible matter as to endanger human life or property — up to 6 months or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Negligent conduct with respect to explosive substance
sections.description: Rashly or negligently handling explosive substances endangering human life — up to 2 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Negligent conduct with respect to machinery
sections.description: Rashly or negligently handling any machinery so as to endanger human life — up to 1 year or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Pulling down, damaging or interfering with any work of irrigation, navigation or drinking water
sections.description: Pulling down, damaging or interfering with any work of irrigation, navigation, embankment or drinking water supply — up to 5 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Negligent conduct with respect to pulling down or repairing buildings
sections.description: Rashly or negligently omitting to take necessary precaution during pulling down or repairing buildings so as to endanger human life — up to 1 year or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Causing disappearance of evidence of any other offence to screen offender
sections.description: Causing disappearance of evidence of any offence with intention of screening the offender from legal punishment — punishable as per offence screened.

sections.section_title: Culpable homicide
sections.description: Causing death by doing an act with intention or knowledge that death or grievous hurt is the likely consequence. Not amounting to murder if specific exceptions apply.

sections.section_title: Murder
sections.description: Culpable homicide is murder when done with intention of causing death, or grievous bodily injury likely to cause death, or with knowledge that death will likely result, or imminently dangerous act.

sections.section_title: Punishment for murder
sections.description: Death or imprisonment for life, and fine. Death penalty mandatory for murder committed by life convict.

sections.section_title: Punishment for culpable homicide not amounting to murder
sections.description: Imprisonment for life or up to 10 years (if done with intent to cause death) or up to 10 years (if without intention but with knowledge).

sections.section_title: Punishment for causing death with rash or negligent act
sections.description: Causing death by doing a rash or negligent act not amounting to culpable homicide — up to 5 years and fine. Hit and run: up to 10 years.

sections.section_title: Abetment of suicide
sections.description: Abetting the commission of suicide — if suicide committed, up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Attempt to commit suicide to compel or restrain exercise of lawful power
sections.description: Attempting suicide to compel or restrain government servant from performing lawful duty — up to 1 year or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Causing miscarriage
sections.description: Voluntarily causing a woman with child to miscarry — up to 3 years or fine or both; if without woman's consent, up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Causing miscarriage without woman's consent
sections.description: Causing miscarriage without the consent of the woman — up to life imprisonment or 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Death caused by act done with intent to cause miscarriage
sections.description: If act done to cause miscarriage results in woman's death — up to 10 years or, if without consent, imprisonment for life and fine.

sections.section_title: Act done with intent to prevent child being born alive, or to cause it to die after birth
sections.description: Doing any act before or during birth of a child with intent to prevent it from being born alive or to cause it to die after birth — up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Exposure and abandonment of child under twelve years, by parent or person having care of it
sections.description: Parent or person having care of a child under 12 exposing or abandoning the child with intent to abandon — up to 7 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Concealment of birth by secret disposal of dead body
sections.description: Secretly disposing of the dead body of a child, whether born dead or having died after birth, with intent to conceal the birth — up to 2 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Terrorist act
sections.description: New terrorism provision in BNS — doing any act with intent to threaten the unity, integrity, sovereignty, security of India or strike terror in people, causing death/injury/property destruction using weapons/explosives.

sections.section_title: Punishment for terrorist acts
sections.description: Punishment for terrorist acts resulting in death — death or life imprisonment. Other cases: life imprisonment or up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt
sections.description: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Voluntarily causing hurt by dangerous weapons or means
sections.description: Voluntarily causing hurt by means of fire/heat/corrosive substance/poison/explosion/etc. — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt by dangerous weapons or means
sections.description: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt by dangerous weapon — up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Voluntarily causing hurt to extort property, or to constrain to an illegal act
sections.description: Voluntarily causing hurt for the purpose of extorting property or forcing a person into doing a legal act or illegal one — up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt to extort property
sections.description: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt to extort property or compelling a person to do something — imprisonment for life or up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Voluntarily causing hurt to extort confession
sections.description: Voluntarily causing hurt to extort from the sufferer or any other person any confession or information to detect/punish an offence — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt to extort confession or information
sections.description: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt to extort confession — imprisonment for life or up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Causing hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others
sections.description: Causing hurt to a person by a rash or negligent act that endangers human life or personal safety — up to 1 year or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Causing grievous hurt by act endangering life or personal safety
sections.description: Grievous hurt by rash or negligent act endangering life — up to 2 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Act endangering life or personal safety of others
sections.description: Rashly or negligently doing any act which endangers human life or personal safety of others — up to 3 months or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Wrongful restraint
sections.description: Voluntarily obstructing any person so as to prevent them from proceeding in any direction — up to 1 month or fine.

sections.section_title: Wrongful confinement
sections.description: Wrongfully confining a person so as to prevent them from proceeding beyond certain circumscribing limits — up to 1 year or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Wrongful confinement for three or more days
sections.description: Wrongful confinement for 3 or more days — up to 2 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Wrongful confinement for ten or more days
sections.description: Wrongful confinement for 10 or more days — up to 3 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Wrongful confinement of person for whose liberation writ has been issued
sections.description: Wrongfully confining a person knowing a writ for their liberation has been issued — up to 2 years in addition to imprisonment for original confinement.

sections.section_title: Wrongful confinement in secret
sections.description: Wrongful confinement in any place where the person confined is in the knowledge of no one except the person confining — up to 2 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Wrongful confinement to extort property or to constrain to illegal act
sections.description: Confining a person to extort from that person or any person interested property or valuable security or to force commission of illegal act — up to 3 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Wrongful confinement to extort confession or compel restoration of property
sections.description: Wrongful confinement to extort confession or compel restoration of property — up to 3 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Assault
sections.description: Making any gesture or preparation intending or knowing it to be likely that such gesture/preparation will cause any person apprehension that criminal force is about to be used — up to 3 months or fine.

sections.section_title: Criminal force
sections.description: Intentionally using force on another person without that person's consent, intending or knowing it is likely to cause injury, fear or annoyance — up to 3 months or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Assault or criminal force to deter public servant from discharge of his duty
sections.description: Assault or criminal force used to deter a public servant from discharging duty — up to 2 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Assault or criminal force with intent to dishonour person
sections.description: Assault or criminal force intending to dishonour a person — up to 2 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Assault or criminal force in attempt to commit theft of property carried by a person
sections.description: Assault or criminal force attempting to commit theft of property carried by a person — up to 2 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Assault or criminal force in attempt wrongfully to confine a person
sections.description: Assault or criminal force attempting to wrongfully confine a person — up to 1 year and fine.

sections.section_title: Assault or criminal force on grave provocation
sections.description: Assault or criminal force committed on grave and sudden provocation — up to 1 month or fine or both. Mitigated offence.

sections.section_title: Kidnapping
sections.description: Kidnapping from India — taking or enticing any person from India without consent of government. Kidnapping from lawful guardianship — taking minor or unsound mind person.

sections.section_title: Abduction
sections.description: Whoever by force or compulsion or by deceitful means induces any person to go from any place — that person is said to abduct the other.

sections.section_title: Kidnapping or maiming a minor for purposes of begging
sections.description: Kidnapping or maiming a minor under 18 for begging — imprisonment for life or up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Kidnapping or abducting in order to murder
sections.description: Kidnapping or abducting any person in order that such person may be murdered or disposed of so as to be put in danger of being murdered — imprisonment for life or up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Kidnapping for ransom, etc.
sections.description: Kidnapping or abducting any person and keeping them in confinement, demanding ransom or to cause death/hurt or to wrongfully confine — death or imprisonment for life and fine.

sections.section_title: Kidnapping or abducting with intent secretly and wrongfully to confine a person
sections.description: Kidnapping or abducting a person with intent to secretly and wrongfully confine — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Abduction of person to subject to grievous hurt, slavery, etc.
sections.description: Kidnapping or abducting to subject the person to grievous hurt, slavery, unnatural lust — up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Rape
sections.description: Sexual intercourse without consent or with consent obtained by fear, fraud, intoxication, or when woman is under 18. Punishable with 10 years to life imprisonment.

sections.section_title: Punishment for rape
sections.description: Rape: up to 10 years rigorous imprisonment and fine. Rape by police/public servant/armed forces/hospital staff: 10 years to life. Gang rape: 20 years to life or death.

sections.section_title: Punishment for rape on woman under sixteen years of age
sections.description: Rape on woman under 16 — minimum 20 years rigorous imprisonment, may extend to life (remainder of natural life).

sections.section_title: Sexual intercourse by husband upon his wife during separation
sections.description: Sexual intercourse by husband on his wife while living separately under decree of separation — up to 2 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Sexual intercourse by a person in authority
sections.description: Sexual intercourse by person in position of authority — superintendent of jail, remand home, hospital staff, custodian — on any woman in their charge — up to 5 to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Gang rape
sections.description: When rape is committed by one or more persons constituting a group or acting in furtherance of a common intention — each person deemed to have committed rape. Up to 20 years or life.

sections.section_title: Rape by a person who is related to victim
sections.description: Rape committed by husband on his own wife — if wife is under 18, imprisonment up to 10 years. Special provision for marital rape of minor.

sections.section_title: Punishment for repeat offenders
sections.description: Whoever having been previously convicted of an offence punishable under provisions relating to rape is again convicted — imprisonment for life (remainder of natural life).

sections.section_title: Disclosure of identity of victim of certain offences
sections.description: Printing or publishing the name or any matter which may make known the identity of any person against whom rape or sexual offence is alleged — up to 2 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Punishment for causing death or resulting in persistent vegetative state of victim
sections.description: Offences under rape sections causing death or resulting in persistent vegetative state — death or life imprisonment (remainder of natural life) and fine.

sections.section_title: Acid attack
sections.description: Throwing or administering acid or attempting to do so — with intent or knowledge that grievous hurt will be caused. Imprisonment up to 10 years to life and fine (minimum Rs. 10 lakhs).

sections.section_title: Attempt to commit acid attack
sections.description: Attempt to throw or administer acid — up to 5 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Sexual harassment
sections.description: Making unwelcome physical contact/advances, demanding sexual favours, showing pornography, making sexually coloured remarks — up to 3 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Assault or use of criminal force to woman with intent to outrage her modesty
sections.description: Assault or criminal force to any woman, intending to outrage or knowing it will outrage her modesty — up to 2 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Voyeurism
sections.description: Watching or capturing image of a woman engaged in private act where she would have expectation of privacy, disseminating or threatening to disseminate — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Stalking
sections.description: Following a woman, contacting her or monitoring her use of internet or email despite clear indication of disinterest — up to 3 years for first offence, up to 5 years for subsequent offence.

sections.section_title: Word, gesture or act intended to insult the modesty of a woman
sections.description: Any word, sound, gesture or object intending to insult the modesty of a woman — up to 3 years or fine or both (1 year in public places).

sections.section_title: Dowry death
sections.description: Where death of woman caused by burns/bodily injury or occurs under abnormal circumstances within 7 years of marriage, and there was cruelty/harassment for dowry — punishable with 7 years to life.

sections.section_title: Importation of girls from foreign country
sections.description: Importing into India from any country outside India any girl under 21 for illicit intercourse — up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Selling minor for purposes of prostitution, etc.
sections.description: Selling, letting for hire, or otherwise disposing of any person under 18 with intent that they may be used for prostitution or illicit intercourse — up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Buying minor for purposes of prostitution, etc.
sections.description: Buying, hiring or obtaining possession of any person under 18 for purpose of prostitution or illicit intercourse — up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Cruelty by husband or his relatives
sections.description: Husband or his relatives subjecting woman to cruelty — wilful conduct driving woman to suicide or gravely endangering her life or harassing for unlawful dowry demands — up to 3 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Husband or his relatives subjected a woman to cruelty
sections.description: Provision detailing dowry harassment and cruelty by husband or in-laws — related provisions for cognisance and punishment.

sections.section_title: Enticing or taking away or detaining with criminal intent a married woman
sections.description: Taking or enticing away any woman who is and whom the offender knows to be the wife of another man with intent that she may have illicit intercourse — up to 2 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Marrying again during lifetime of husband or wife
sections.description: Marrying again during the lifetime of one's spouse without lawful cause — up to 7 years and fine. Bigamy offence.

sections.section_title: Marriage ceremony fraudulently gone through without lawful marriage
sections.description: Dishonestly or fraudulently going through a ceremony of being married, knowing that they are not thereby lawfully married — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Cohabitation caused by a man deceitfully inducing a belief of lawful marriage
sections.description: Man deceitfully inducing a woman to believe she is lawfully married and cohabiting with her under that false belief — up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Misconduct in public by a drunken person
sections.description: Being drunk and causing annoyance to any person, or appearing drunk in any public place in indecent state or incapable of taking care of themselves — up to 24 hours detention or fine.

sections.section_title: Trafficking of person
sections.description: Trafficking of a person for exploitation including sexual exploitation, forced labour, slavery, organ removal — up to 7 years; if child, up to 10 years; if more than one person, up to 14 years; habitual offender, life.

sections.section_title: Exploitation of a trafficked person
sections.description: Causing a trafficked person to perform forced labour, bonded labour, sexual exploitation, organ extraction etc. — up to 5 years; if child, up to 7 years.

sections.section_title: Habitual dealing in slaves
sections.description: Habitual dealing in slaves — imprisonment for life or up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Unlawful compulsory labour
sections.description: Compelling any person to labour against their will — up to 1 year or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Theft
sections.description: Dishonestly taking any movable property out of the possession of any person without that person's consent — up to 3 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Theft in dwelling house, etc.
sections.description: Theft in a building, tent or vessel used as human dwelling, or place of worship, or store of property — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Theft by clerk or servant
sections.description: Theft by a clerk or servant of property in possession of their master — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Theft after preparation made for causing death, hurt or restraint in order to committing of the theft
sections.description: Theft after preparation by the thief to use death-causing force or wrongful restraint — up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Extortion
sections.description: Putting any person in fear of injury and thereby dishonestly inducing them to deliver any property or valuable security — up to 3 years and fine or both.

sections.section_title: Robbery
sections.description: Theft or extortion accompanied by or resulting from voluntarily causing or attempting to cause death, hurt or wrongful restraint to any person — up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Dacoity
sections.description: When robbery is committed by 5 or more persons conjointly — dacoity. Up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Robbery or dacoity with attempt to cause death or grievous hurt
sections.description: Committing or attempting to commit robbery/dacoity with voluntary causing or attempt to cause death or grievous hurt — imprisonment for life or up to 10 years.

sections.section_title: Dacoity with murder
sections.description: Committing dacoity and murder together — death or imprisonment for life.

sections.section_title: Belonging to gang of dacoits
sections.description: Belonging to a gang of persons associated for the purpose of habitually committing dacoity — imprisonment for life or up to 10 years.

sections.section_title: Possession of stolen property
sections.description: Receiving or retaining any stolen property knowing or having reason to believe it to be stolen — up to 3 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Habitually dealing in stolen property
sections.description: Habitually receiving, retaining or dealing in stolen property — up to 10 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Dishonestly receiving stolen property
sections.description: Dishonestly receiving stolen property knowing it to be stolen — same punishment as theft.

sections.section_title: Cheating
sections.description: Fraudulently or dishonestly inducing any person to deliver property or to consent to retain property, or to do or omit to do anything — up to 3 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Cheating by personation
sections.description: Cheating by pretending to be some other person or by knowingly substituting one person for another — up to 5 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Cheating with knowledge that wrongful loss may ensue to person whose interest offender is bound to protect
sections.description: Cheating with knowledge that wrongful loss may result to a person whose interest the offender is bound to protect — up to 3 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property
sections.description: Cheating and thereby dishonestly inducing delivery of any property or the making, alteration or destruction of any valuable security — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Mischief
sections.description: Causing destruction of or damage to any property with intent to cause wrongful loss or damage — up to 3 months or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Mischief causing damage to the amount of five hundred rupees
sections.description: Mischief causing damage of Rs. 500 or more — up to 2 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Mischief by killing or maiming animal of the value of fifty rupees
sections.description: Mischief by killing, poisoning, maiming or rendering useless any animal — up to 5 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Mischief by injury to works of irrigation or by wrongfully diverting water
sections.description: Mischief by injuring works of irrigation or supply of water to public — up to 5 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Mischief by injury to public road, bridge, river or channel
sections.description: Mischief by damage to public road, bridge, river, channel, or another public utility — up to 5 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Mischief by injury to telegraph line
sections.description: Mischief by injury to telegraph line or any public communication/utility line — up to 5 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Mischief by causing inundation or obstruction to public drainage attended with damage
sections.description: Causing inundation, overflow or drainage obstruction resulting in damage — up to 5 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Mischief by destruction of lighthouse or sea-mark
sections.description: Destroying or rendering useless any lighthouse or other sea-mark used for the guidance of navigation — imprisonment for life or up to 7 years.

sections.section_title: Mischief by destroying or moving or rendering less useful a landmark fixed by public authority
sections.description: Destroying, moving, or rendering useless any landmark fixed by public authority — up to 1 year or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Mischief committed after preparation made for causing death or grievous hurt
sections.description: Mischief committed after prior preparation to cause death, hurt or wrongful restraint to prevent the mischief's interruption — up to 5 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Criminal trespass
sections.description: Entering into or upon property in possession of another with intent to commit an offence or to intimidate, insult or annoy any person in possession — up to 3 months or fine or both.

sections.section_title: House-trespass
sections.description: Criminal trespass in any building, tent or vessel used as human dwelling, place of worship, or storing custody of property — up to 1 year and fine.

sections.section_title: Lurking house-trespass or house-breaking
sections.description: House-trespass by concealment (lurking) or house-breaking — up to 2 years and fine.

sections.section_title: House-breaking by night
sections.description: House-breaking between sunset and sunrise — up to 3 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Forgery
sections.description: Making a false document or false electronic record intending that it be used to cause damage or injury, support a fraudulent claim, commit fraud or be used as evidence — up to 2 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Forgery of record of Court or of public register, etc.
sections.description: Forgery of a court record, register, deed, or official document — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Forgery of valuable security, will, etc.
sections.description: Forgery of a valuable security, will or authority to make over property — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Forgery for purpose of cheating
sections.description: Forgery committed with intent to cheat — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Forgery for purpose of harming reputation
sections.description: Forgery with intent to harm any person's reputation — up to 3 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Using as genuine a forged document or forged electronic record
sections.description: Using as genuine any forged document or electronic record — same punishment as forgery.

sections.section_title: Making or possessing counterfeit seal, plate, etc., with intent to commit forgery punishable under section 329
sections.description: Making/possessing counterfeit seal or instrument intending forgery of official documents — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Counterfeiting device or mark used for authenticating documents described in section 330, and possession
sections.description: Counterfeiting device/mark used for authenticating valuable securities — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Fraudulent cancellation, destruction, etc., of will, authority to adopt, or valuable security
sections.description: Fraudulently cancelling, destroying, or altering any will, authority to adopt, or valuable security — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Falsification of accounts
sections.description: Willfully and with intent to defraud destroying, altering, mutilating, falsifying any book, account, document or valuable security — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Property mark
sections.description: Making a false property mark (a mark used to denote ownership of property) intending to cause wrongful loss or gain — up to 1 year or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Tampering with property mark with intent to cause injury
sections.description: Removing, concealing, destroying, altering or obliterating any property mark intending to cause wrongful loss — up to 1 year or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Making a false mark upon any receptacle containing goods
sections.description: Marking false description on any package or receptacle containing goods — up to 1 year or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Punishment for making use of any such false mark
sections.description: Using any package or receptacle marked with a false property mark — up to 1 year or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Selling goods marked with a counterfeit property mark
sections.description: Selling goods bearing a counterfeit property mark — up to 1 year or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Making or possessing instruments for counterfeiting a property mark
sections.description: Making or possessing any instrument for counterfeiting a property mark — up to 3 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Other offences relating to property mark
sections.description: Miscellaneous property mark offences — up to 1 year or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Fraudulent possession of property marks
sections.description: Fraudulently possessing property marks used by others — up to 1 year or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Criminal breach of trust
sections.description: Whoever, being in any manner entrusted with property, dishonestly misappropriates or converts it for their own use, or dishonestly disposes of it — up to 3 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Fraudulent removal or concealment of property to prevent distribution among creditors
sections.description: Fraudulently removing, concealing or delivering up property to prevent distribution among creditors — up to 2 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Fraudulently preventing from being made available for creditors a debt or demand due to the offender
sections.description: Fraudulently accepting satisfaction for any debt due to oneself to prevent it becoming available to creditors — up to 2 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Fraudulent execution of deed of transfer containing false statement of consideration
sections.description: Executing a deed of transfer of property with false statement of consideration — up to 2 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Fraudulent removal of property
sections.description: Fraudulently removing or concealing any property intending to prevent attachment, distress or sale of that property — up to 2 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Dishonest misappropriation of property
sections.description: Dishonestly misappropriating or converting to own use any movable property — up to 2 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Dishonest misappropriation of property possessed by deceased person at the time of his death
sections.description: Misappropriating property that was in possession of deceased at time of death — up to 3 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Punishment for criminal breach of trust
sections.description: Punishment for criminal breach of trust as servant, clerk, carrier, warehouseman, wharfinger, banker, merchant, factor, broker, attorney, agent — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Criminal breach of trust by carrier, wharfinger, etc.
sections.description: Criminal breach of trust by carrier, wharfinger, warehouseman — up to 7 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Receiving property stolen in the commission of a dacoity
sections.description: Receiving or retaining property knowing it to have been stolen in a dacoity — up to life imprisonment or 10 years.

sections.section_title: Defamation
sections.description: Making or publishing any imputation concerning a person intending to harm or knowing it likely to harm their reputation — up to 2 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Punishment for defamation
sections.description: Defamation punishment: up to 2 years or fine or both; defamation of class — up to 2 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Criminal intimidation
sections.description: Threatening any person with injury to person, reputation or property of that person or their family, to cause alarm or compel that person to do or omit to do any act — up to 2 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Intentional insult with intent to provoke breach of the peace
sections.description: Intentionally insulting a person with intent to provoke them to break the peace — up to 2 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Punishment for offences committed by armed forces on civilians
