sections.description: Provisions for armed forces personnel committing offences on civilians — jurisdiction and punishment.

sections.section_title: Public servant unlawfully buying or bidding for property
sections.description: Public servant unlawfully buying or bidding for property subject to sale by their official authority — up to 2 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Omission to produce document or electronic record to public servant by person legally bound to produce it
sections.description: Failing to produce document or electronic record demanded by public servant — up to 1 month or Rs. 500 fine.

sections.section_title: Omission to give notice or information to public servant by person legally bound to give it
sections.description: Omitting to give notice or information as required by law — up to 1 month or fine.

sections.section_title: Furnishing false information
sections.description: Furnishing false information to a public servant — up to 6 months or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Refusing oath or affirmation when duly required by public servant to take oath
sections.description: Refusing to bind oneself by oath or solemn affirmation when legally required — up to 6 months or fine.

sections.section_title: Refusing to answer question asked by public servant authorised to question
sections.description: Refusing to answer question put by a public servant legally authorised — up to 6 months or fine.

sections.section_title: Refusing to sign statement
sections.description: Refusing to sign a statement when legally required — up to 3 months or fine.

sections.section_title: False statement on oath or affirmation to public servant
sections.description: Making false statement on oath to public servant or authorised person — up to 3 years and fine.

sections.section_title: False information with intent to cause public servant to use his lawful power to the injury of another
sections.description: Giving false information to public servant to misuse official power against another — up to 1 year or fine.

sections.section_title: Resistance to the taking of property by the lawful authority of a public servant
sections.description: Resisting seizure of property by public servant — up to 6 months or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Obstructing sale of property offered for sale by authority of public servant
sections.description: Obstructing official auction sale — up to 1 month or Rs. 500 fine.

sections.section_title: Illegal purchase or bid for property offered for sale by authority of public servant
sections.description: Illegal bidding at official auction by prohibited person — up to 1 month or fine.

sections.section_title: Obstructing public servant in discharge of public functions
sections.description: Obstructing public servant in discharge of official functions — up to 3 months or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Omission to assist public servant when bound by law to give assistance
sections.description: Failing to assist public servant when legally required — up to 1 month or fine.

sections.section_title: Disobedience to order duly promulgated by public servant
sections.description: Disobeying promulgated public order — up to 6 months or fine or both. Section 144 CrPC equivalent.

sections.section_title: Unlawful return from transportation — additional
sections.description: Supplementary provision for unlawful return from transportation or deportation.

sections.section_title: Snatching
sections.description: New BNS provision for snatching — taking movable property suddenly or quickly from the possession of another — up to 3 years and fine.

sections.section_title: Act done by several persons in furtherance of common intention
sections.description: When a criminal act is done by several persons in furtherance of common intention, each is liable as if the act was done by them alone. Equivalent to IPC Section 34.

sections.section_title: Authorship and Ownership of Copyright
sections.description: Establishes who owns the copyright in original literary, dramatic, musical, artistic works and cinematograph films. First owner is the author unless created under employment/contract.

sections.section_title: Copyright Infringement
sections.description: Defines when copyright is infringed — doing any act that only the copyright owner is authorised to do, or permitting any place to be used for infringing communication to the public.

sections.section_title: Civil Remedies for Copyright Infringement
sections.description: Grants civil remedies including injunction, damages, accounts of profits and delivery up of infringing copies to the copyright owner.

sections.section_title: Criminal Offence for Copyright Infringement
sections.description: Makes knowingly infringing or abetting infringement of copyright a criminal offence punishable with imprisonment and fine.

sections.section_title: Patent Grant and Rights
sections.description: Grants patentee the exclusive right to prevent third parties from making, using, offering for sale, selling or importing the patented product or process without consent.

sections.section_title: Patent Infringement Suit
sections.description: Governs suits for infringement of patents including jurisdiction, relief available and counter-claim for revocation.

sections.section_title: Certain Acts Not Infringement (Bolar / Parallel Import)
sections.description: Permits acts done for research, experiment, regulatory approval (Bolar exemption) and importation of patented products by authorised person (parallel import).

sections.section_title: Trademark Infringement
sections.description: Defines infringement of registered trademark — using identical or deceptively similar mark in the course of trade for same or similar goods/services.

sections.section_title: Suit for Infringement / Passing Off
sections.description: Jurisdiction for suits relating to infringement of registered trademark or for passing off. Such suits to be filed in District Court.

sections.section_title: Criminal Penalty for Trademark Offences
sections.description: Imposes criminal penalty for applying false trade marks, trade descriptions, selling goods with false marks, and counterfeiting.

sections.section_title: Design Infringement
sections.description: Infringement of registered design by applying the design or fraudulent imitation to any article for sale without the registered proprietor's licence.

sections.section_title: Geographical Indication Infringement
sections.law_reference: Geographical Indications of Goods Act 1999, Section 22
sections.description: Infringement of registered geographical indication by using false GI in goods not originating from the indicated region.

sections.section_title: IP Ownership in Employment Contract
sections.law_reference: Copyright Act S.17 / Patents Act S.6 / Contract Act
sections.description: Clause assigning all IP created by employee during employment to the employer. Covers inventions, software, designs, content created in the course of employment.

sections.section_title: IP Licensing Clause
sections.law_reference: Copyright Act S.30 / Patents Act S.68 / Contract Act
sections.description: Grants a licensee the right to use IP (patent, copyright, trademark, trade secret) under specified terms, territory, duration and exclusivity.

sections.section_title: Trade Secret and Confidential Information Protection
sections.law_reference: Contract Act 1872 / IT Act 2000 / IPC S.408
sections.description: Protects proprietary business information, formulas, processes, designs, customer lists, and know-how from unauthorised disclosure or use.

sections.section_title: Non-Compete and Non-Solicitation (IP Context)
sections.description: Restricts employee/contractor from working for competitors or soliciting clients/employees for a specified period and geography after termination — to protect IP and confidential information.

sections.section_title: Technology Transfer Agreement
sections.law_reference: Patents Act / FEMA / IT Act / Contract Act
sections.description: Agreement for transfer of patented technology, technical know-how or software from one party to another, including terms of royalty, duration and sublicensing rights.

sections.section_title: Criminal Breach of Trust (IPC)
sections.description: Punishment for criminal breach of trust. Whoever commits criminal breach of trust shall be punished with imprisonment of up to 3 years or fine or both.

sections.section_title: Cheating and Dishonestly Inducing Delivery (IPC)
sections.description: Classic cheating provision — deceiving a person to deliver property or alter signed/sealed document. IPC 420 remains widely referenced in legal contracts.

sections.section_title: Forgery (IPC)
sections.description: Forgery of a document to cause damage or injury, support a claim or title, or commit fraud. IPC 463/465 widely cited in contract disputes.

sections.section_title: Defamation (IPC)
sections.description: Defamation by imputation — making or publishing any imputation concerning a person intending to harm their reputation. Relevant to IP, brand and contract disputes.

sections.section_title: Cheating (IPC)
sections.description: Cheating by fraudulently inducing a person to deliver property or to do or omit to do anything which the person would not do/omit but for the deception.

sections.section_title: Criminal Trespass (IPC)
sections.description: Entering into or upon property in possession of another with intent to commit an offence or to intimidate, insult or annoy any person in possession.

sections.section_title: Criminal Conspiracy (IPC)
sections.description: Criminal conspiracy to commit a crime punishable with death, life imprisonment or rigorous imprisonment ≥2 years. Agreement between two or more persons to do an illegal act.

sections.section_title: Acts Done in Furtherance of Common Intention (IPC)
sections.description: When a criminal act is done by several persons in furtherance of the common intention of all, each of such persons is liable for that act as if done by them alone.

sections.section_title: Perjury / False Evidence (IPC)
sections.description: Giving false evidence in judicial proceeding or fabricating false evidence for purpose of any proceeding, whether judicial or otherwise.

sections.section_title: Public Nuisance (IPC)
sections.description: Act or illegal omission causing common injury, danger or annoyance to the public or people in general who dwell or occupy property in the vicinity.

sections.section_title: EPF Contribution Obligation
sections.description: Mandatory contribution by employer (12% of basic wages) and employee (12%) to the Employees' Provident Fund. Applies to establishments with 20+ employees.

sections.section_title: Penalty for EPF Default
sections.description: Damages for default in payment of EPF contributions — up to 25% per annum for default up to 2 months, up to 37% for 2–4 months, and 50% beyond 6 months.

sections.section_title: Employees' Pension Scheme
sections.description: Pension scheme for employees — employer contributes 8.33% of wages to EPS-95. Provides for superannuation, disability and family pension.

sections.section_title: ESI Contribution (Employee State Insurance)
sections.description: Mandatory ESI contribution by employer (3.25% of wages) and employee (0.75%) for health insurance and sickness benefits. Applies to establishments with 10+ employees.

sections.section_title: Gratuity Payment Obligation
sections.description: Every employee who has completed 5 years of continuous service is entitled to gratuity at the rate of 15 days wages for each completed year of service.

sections.section_title: Minimum Wages Compliance
sections.law_reference: Minimum Wages Act 1948 / Code on Wages 2019
sections.description: Employer obligation to pay not less than the minimum wage fixed by the government for scheduled employments. Non-payment is a criminal offence.

sections.section_title: Statutory Bonus
sections.description: Every employer is bound to pay a minimum bonus of 8.33% of annual wages to every employee, regardless of profit or loss, subject to eligibility criteria.

sections.section_title: Maternity Benefit
sections.description: Every woman employee is entitled to 26 weeks of paid maternity leave (12 weeks for 3rd child). Employer cannot dismiss or discharge a woman during maternity.

sections.section_title: Prevention of Sexual Harassment at Workplace (POSH)
sections.law_reference: Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace Act 2013, Section 4
sections.description: Mandatory constitution of Internal Complaints Committee (ICC) at every workplace with 10+ employees. Employer must conduct awareness programmes and display policy.

sections.section_title: Retrenchment Compensation
sections.description: Conditions precedent to retrenchment — employer must give 1 month notice or pay in lieu, pay retrenchment compensation at 15 days average pay per year of service.

sections.section_title: Contract Labour Regulation and Abolition
sections.description: Regulates employment of contract labour and provides for its abolition in certain circumstances. Principal employer is jointly liable for wages and amenities of contract workers.

sections.section_title: Offer and Acceptance
sections.description: A contract is formed when a valid offer is accepted absolutely and unqualifiedly. Counter-offers amount to rejection. Essential element of contract formation.

sections.section_title: Consideration
sections.description: An agreement without consideration is void unless it falls within specified exceptions. Consideration must be real, lawful and not illusory.

sections.section_title: Compensation for Breach of Contract
sections.description: Party who suffers loss from breach of contract is entitled to compensation for loss or damage arising naturally from the breach. Remote or indirect loss not compensable.

sections.section_title: Liquidated Damages / Penalty Clause
sections.description: When a sum is named as compensation in contract, court may award reasonable compensation not exceeding the named sum. Genuine pre-estimate vs penalty distinction.

sections.section_title: Frustration of Contract
sections.description: Agreement to do an impossible act is void. Contract becomes void if subsequent impossibility of performance arises due to reasons beyond the party's control.

sections.section_title: Force Majeure Clause
sections.description: Excuses a party from performing contractual obligations when prevented by extraordinary circumstances beyond their control — war, natural disaster, pandemic, government action.

sections.section_title: Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA)
sections.description: Binds receiving party to keep confidential information secret and not to use it for any purpose other than the permitted purpose. One-way or mutual NDA.

sections.section_title: Arbitration Clause
sections.description: Agreement by parties to refer disputes arising out of or in connection with the contract to arbitration. Must be in writing. Covers seat, number of arbitrators, rules.

sections.section_title: Indemnity Clause
sections.description: One party (indemnifier) promises to save the other (indemnity holder) from loss caused by the indemnifier's conduct or by conduct of any third party.

sections.section_title: Limitation of Liability Clause
sections.law_reference: Indian Contract Act 1872 / Specific Relief Act
sections.description: Caps the maximum liability of a party under the contract. Often expressed as a multiple of fees paid. May exclude consequential, indirect, special or punitive damages.

sections.section_title: Termination Clause
sections.description: Specifies conditions under which either party may terminate the contract — for cause (material breach, insolvency), for convenience (notice), or by mutual consent.

sections.section_title: Governing Law and Jurisdiction Clause
sections.law_reference: Civil Procedure Code 1908 / Private International Law
sections.description: Specifies which country's/state's law governs the contract and which courts have jurisdiction to resolve disputes. Critical in cross-border contracts.

sections.section_title: Assignment and Novation Clause
sections.description: Restricts or permits transfer of contractual rights and obligations. Novation discharges original contract and replaces with new one involving substituted party.

sections.section_title: Entire Agreement / Merger Clause
sections.description: States that the written contract constitutes the entire agreement between parties, superseding all prior negotiations, representations and understandings.

sections.section_title: Representations and Warranties
sections.law_reference: Indian Contract Act 1872 / Sale of Goods Act 1930
sections.description: Statements of fact made by a party that are true at the time of contracting. Breach of warranty entitles other party to damages. Distinguished from conditions.

sections.section_title: Amendment and Modification Clause
sections.description: Any amendment, modification or waiver of the contract must be in writing and signed by authorised representatives of both parties.

sections.section_title: Severability Clause
sections.description: If any provision of the contract is found invalid or unenforceable, that provision is severed and the remaining contract continues in full force and effect.

sections.section_title: Waiver Clause
sections.description: Failure to exercise or delay in exercising a right does not constitute waiver. Waiver of breach on one occasion does not waive future breaches.

sections.section_title: Notice Clause
sections.description: Prescribes the manner in which formal notices under the contract must be given — by registered post, courier, email with acknowledgement, or personal delivery — and when notice is deemed received.

sections.section_title: Specific Performance
sections.description: Court may order specific performance of a contract where monetary compensation is not adequate remedy — e.g., unique property, IP licensing, unique goods.

sections.section_title: Foreign Exchange and Cross-Border Payment
sections.description: Governs cross-border payments under international contracts — royalties, service fees, technology transfer payments — requiring RBI approval or reporting under FEMA.

sections.section_title: Data Protection and Privacy Clause
sections.law_reference: IT Act 2000 / DPDP Act 2023 / Contract Act
sections.description: Governs collection, processing, storage and transfer of personal data under a contract. Requires consent, data minimisation, purpose limitation and security measures.

sections.section_title: Service Level Agreement (SLA)
sections.description: Defines the level of service to be provided — uptime, response time, resolution time — and remedies for failure to meet SLAs including service credits.

sections.section_title: Escrow Arrangement
sections.law_reference: Indian Contract Act 1872 / Specific Relief Act
sections.description: Arrangement where a third party holds funds, documents or assets on behalf of contracting parties until specified conditions are met, often used in M&A and software licensing.

sections.section_title: Change of Control Clause
sections.description: Gives a party the right to terminate or renegotiate the contract if the other party undergoes a change of control (merger, acquisition, majority shareholding change).

sections.section_title: Step-In Rights
sections.description: Allows a party (often lender or government) to take over performance of a defaulting contractor's obligations to ensure project continuity — common in PPP and infrastructure contracts.

sections.section_title: GST Liability and Indemnity
sections.description: Contract clause allocating GST liability between parties — which party bears GST, how invoices are issued, and indemnity for GST default by vendor/service provider.

sections.section_title: Stamp Duty on Contracts
sections.law_reference: Indian Stamp Act 1899 / State Stamp Acts
sections.description: Contracts must be adequately stamped to be admissible in evidence. Unstamped/insufficiently stamped documents can be impounded and are unenforceable.

sections.section_title: Board Authority to Enter Contracts
sections.description: Board of Directors powers to enter contracts on behalf of the company. Certain transactions require shareholder approval. Ultra vires contracts are void.

sections.section_title: Moratorium / Insolvency Clause
sections.description: Upon commencement of CIRP, moratorium is declared — no suits, proceedings, recovery of property, or enforcement of security interests against the corporate debtor.

sections.section_title: Environmental Compliance Clause
sections.description: Obligates contracting parties to comply with all applicable environmental laws. Includes liability for environmental damage, pollution indemnity and NGT compliance.

sections.section_title: Anti-Bribery and Anti-Corruption (ABAC) Clause
sections.law_reference: Prevention of Corruption Act 1988 / FCPA / UK Bribery Act
sections.description: Prohibits parties from paying or receiving bribes in connection with the contract. Requires compliance with anti-corruption laws including FCPA and UK Bribery Act for international contracts.

sections.section_title: Sanctions Compliance Clause
sections.description: Prohibits parties from engaging in transactions with sanctioned countries, entities or individuals. Breach entitles immediate termination and indemnity.

sections.section_title: Consumer Protection / Product Liability
sections.description: Liability of manufacturers, sellers and service providers for defective products or deficient services. Includes product recall obligations and consumer redress.

sections.section_title: Competition Law Compliance
sections.description: Prohibits anti-competitive agreements (price fixing, market sharing, bid rigging) and abuse of dominant position. Contracts with competition law violations are void.

sections.section_title: RERA Compliance (Real Estate)
sections.description: Real estate developer obligations under RERA — project registration, disclosure, escrow of 70% funds, defect liability for 5 years, and compensation for delay.
